sections.context: The web_search tool's execute function currently mixes Tavily-specific HTTP transport, payload construction, error handling, parameter normalization, and Markdown formatting in one place. This makes the code shallow, hard to test without mocking global fetch, and impossible to swap search providers without editing the tool. Introducing a SearchProvider seam hides Tavily behind an adapter and unifies the production and test surfaces.
sections.behavior_invariant: After the refactor: the tool must still throw a clear error when TAVILY_API_KEY is missing; clamp maxResults to the range 1-10 with a default of 5; default searchDepth to 'basic' and includeAnswer to true; call the Tavily endpoint with the same JSON payload; surface Tavily HTTP errors as 'Tavily API error (<status>): <body>'; return identical Markdown/text content for successful results, empty results, and answer-prefixed results; and keep the /web-search-status command unchanged.
sections.approach: Define a provider-agnostic SearchProvider interface using explicit SearchRequest and SearchResponse types. Implement TavilySearchProvider to encapsulate the URL, payload, fetch call, and response parsing. Provide an InMemorySearchProvider test adapter so tests cross the same seam as production. Refactor the web_search execute function to normalize parameters, delegate to the provider, and format the returned domain response. Migrate tests from global fetch mocking to the in-memory adapter.

steps.title: Define SearchProvider interface and domain types
steps.description: Add SearchProvider, SearchRequest, and SearchResponse types. Place them in src/index.ts or a new src/search-provider.ts module if the interface grows.

steps.title: Implement TavilySearchProvider adapter
steps.description: Move Tavily endpoint, payload construction, fetch call, HTTP error handling, and JSON parsing into a TavilySearchProvider class/function that implements SearchProvider.

steps.title: Implement InMemorySearchProvider test adapter
steps.description: Create a test-only adapter that returns configurable SearchResponse values and errors. Place it in test/support/in-memory-search-provider.ts.

steps.title: Refactor web_search execute to use SearchProvider
steps.description: Update the tool's execute function to normalize params, instantiate TavilySearchProvider using the environment key, call provider.search(request), and format the returned SearchResponse. Keep formatting logic in the tool.

steps.title: Migrate tests from fetch mock to InMemorySearchProvider
steps.description: Replace global fetch mocks with the in-memory adapter. Preserve coverage for missing key, API error, empty results, and successful results.

steps.title: Run validation and review
steps.description: Run npm run typecheck and npm run test. Open a PR from the feature branch.

acceptance: npm run typecheck passes with no errors
npm run test passes with all existing behaviors covered
src/index.ts no longer contains raw Tavily URL or payload strings outside the TavilySearchProvider adapter
A second search provider could be added by implementing SearchProvider without changing the tool

name: SearchProvider seam for web_search
outcomeNote: Implemented SearchProvider seam with TavilySearchProvider and InMemorySearchProvider adapters. All child tasks completed. Validation passed: npm run typecheck, npm run test, npm run build. Branch search-provider-seam pushed and PR #1 opened at https://github.com/davehardy20/pi-web-search/pull/1. Parent epic pi-web-search-76c3 closed.

execution_profile.parallel_subagents: allowed for independent child tasks after dependencies are met
execution_profile.cycle_fix: Revision preserves existing child plan_step_index mapping: step 7 is review for pi-web-search-e999 and step 8 is validation for pi-web-search-68f6. Validation no longer depends on review; orchestrator review gates depend on implementation work automatically.

sections.context: The existing SearchProvider seam successfully hides Tavily transport and response parsing, but readiness and provider lifecycle still leak into the Pi extension shell. src/index.ts checks process.env.TAVILY_API_KEY, the /web-search-status command independently reports Tavily key state, and tests using InMemorySearchProvider still need to set the Tavily environment variable. This makes the SearchProvider seam less deep than intended: provider-specific readiness rules remain in callers instead of behind the seam. Dave selected the search runtime seam and the runtime object interface alternative after the exploratory architecture report.
sections.behavior_invariant: The refactor must preserve the externally visible web_search and /web-search-status behaviours unless a test explicitly documents equivalent wording. web_search must still reject a missing Tavily key with a clear setup message for the default Tavily runtime; normalize maxResults to 1-10 with default 5; default searchDepth to basic and includeAnswer to true; pass AbortSignal through to provider search; preserve successful, empty-result, answer-prefixed, and provider-error output semantics; and keep /web-search-status reporting package metadata plus whether the default Tavily runtime is ready. Tests should no longer need Tavily environment setup when exercising an injected ready in-memory runtime.
sections.approach: Introduce a small SearchRuntime interface with readiness() and search(request) behaviour. Keep SearchProvider as the provider query seam, but add a runtime layer that owns readiness, credential lookup, default Tavily provider creation, and provider invocation semantics. The Pi extension becomes a registration shell that delegates status and search decisions to the runtime. Provide a ready in-memory runtime or runtime factory for tests so missing-config tests target the default runtime, while normal tool behaviour tests cross the same runtime seam without setting Tavily environment variables. Reject alternatives: extending every SearchProvider with readiness would force query adapters to own runtime state; adding only a config resolver would leave the extension orchestrating readiness ordering and provider construction. Do not make validation depend on review; /orch-plan-run automatically schedules review gates after implementation work.

steps.title: Add characterization tests for readiness leakage
steps.description: Before changing behaviour, add or tighten tests showing current missing-key handling, /web-search-status readiness output, provider error wrapping, parameter normalization, and the undesired need to set TAVILY_API_KEY for injected in-memory searches.

steps.title: Define SearchRuntime interface and readiness model
steps.description: Add a runtime interface with readiness() and search(request), plus a small readiness result type that can express ready/not-ready state and the existing Tavily setup message. Include a test runtime or factory shape that can be injected into webSearchExtension.

steps.title: Implement the default Tavily search runtime
steps.description: Move TAVILY_API_KEY lookup, missing-key message ownership, TavilySearchProvider construction, and provider invocation behind a default runtime factory. Preserve TavilySearchProvider as the adapter for endpoint/payload/fetch/response parsing.

steps.title: Refactor Pi extension to delegate readiness and search
steps.description: Update src/index.ts so /web-search-status asks the runtime for readiness and web_search delegates normalized SearchRequest execution to the injected runtime. Keep Pi registration, prompt metadata, and result formatting stable.

steps.title: Migrate tests to the runtime seam
steps.description: Replace environment-coupled injected-provider tests with a ready in-memory runtime. Keep a default-runtime missing-key test for Tavily configuration. Ensure tests cross the runtime seam and still cover success, empty results, API/provider errors, abort propagation where relevant, and status readiness.

steps.title: Update README status/config wording if needed
steps.description: If runtime semantics or /web-search-status wording changes, update README notes and troubleshooting. Do not change package publish/build metadata in this epic.

steps.title: Run read-only review against depth goals
steps.description: Review whether the runtime is deep rather than a pass-through: readiness and provider lifecycle should be local, the extension should not know Tavily credential rules, tests should cross the runtime seam, and no hypothetical one-adapter seam should be introduced beyond the existing test/default adapters.

steps.title: Run validation and prepare PR handoff
steps.description: Run npm run typecheck, npm run test, and npm run build. Confirm intended changes are ready on provider-readiness-seam and summarize PR-first handoff requirements for the lead.

acceptance: The Pi extension no longer directly reads process.env.TAVILY_API_KEY for tool execution or status readiness; Tavily credential lookup is local to the default runtime/provider lifecycle module.
webSearchExtension can be tested with an injected ready runtime without setting TAVILY_API_KEY.
The default Tavily runtime still produces a clear missing-key error and /web-search-status still reports ready/not-ready state for Tavily configuration.
SearchProvider remains the query adapter seam; TavilySearchProvider still owns Tavily endpoint, payload, fetch, response parsing, HTTP error mapping, and abort forwarding.
Existing successful, empty-result, answer-prefixed, parameter-normalization, and provider-error behaviours remain covered by tests.
npm run typecheck, npm run test, and npm run build pass.
Review confirms improved locality, leverage, and test surface, and no ADR is written unless a hard-to-reverse surprising trade-off emerges.
Changes are ready on feature branch provider-readiness-seam for PR-first handoff.
